asset.title: Stack Before After V1
asset.purpose: Narrated explainer episode. Judge story clarity, visual design, proof quality, and whether it fits the README claim.

judge.summary: A well-structured narrated explainer comparing a single-player Streamlit workflow with NodeRoom's collaborative, multi-player environment. It clearly demonstrates the value of shared state and agent review lanes.

featureClarity.evidence: The video clearly contrasts the single-player Streamlit pipeline with NodeRoom's multi-player collaborative canvas.

workflowCompleteness.evidence: Shows the initial setup, execution in both environments, and the final state with agent proposals.

visualDesign.evidence: Clean dark-mode UI with clear visual hierarchy, though transition slides are slightly abrupt.

consistency.evidence: Perfectly aligns with the NodeRoom narrative of collaborative spreadsheets, agents, and live traces.

evidenceQuality.evidence: Features authentic product UI captures showing real-time agent execution and cell-level approvals.

legibility.evidence: Most text and spreadsheet cells are readable, though some smaller UI elements may require zooming on smaller screens.

professionalRelevance.evidence: Highly relevant for engineering and product teams designing collaborative AI agent architectures.

productionHonesty.evidence: Accurately frames the comparison as a structural difference in collaboration layers rather than a difference in raw AI intelligence.

observedEvidence: Agent proposal and cell-level approval flow

defects.observed: The text-only transition slides interrupt the product flow and feel slightly dry.
defects.fix: Consider overlaying the text points directly onto a dimmed version of the UI to maintain visual continuity.

judge.suggestedReadmeCaption: Compare a traditional single-player Streamlit stack with NodeRoom's collaborative substrate, featuring live shared state and inline agent review lanes.
